text: Not that I'm defending "I'm Yours", by Jason Mraz, or anything, but is that song really that smug and self-congratulatory? Just asking. I mean, I've heard the song before, and, yep, it is EASILY Jason Mraz's worst song(not that he was ever the most "interesting" dude, his previous songs were definitely better than "I'm Yours"). But either way, I still agree with you, dude. It's like this crappy song was made just for "Pooping the Charts". I learned all this stuff about Jason Mraz from Todd In The Shadows. Because I had no clue who in the hell Jason Mraz even was before the song "I'm Yours", but he looked familiar, like he'd been around since the 90s, but didn't start being the kind of annoying guy who's always playing his acoustic guitar at a party, until "I'm Yours", and I just wish that John Belushi were still alive, so he could smash the acoustic guitar against a wall, like he did in the beginning of the classic movie "National Lampoon's: Animal House". A movie I didn't see as a kid, because I wasn't born when it came out. I'm only 28. But either way, sorry if this went on too long, but I remember when "I'm Yours" charted. It was a boring, tedious, and slow excuse of a summer vacation song, even back then. So, yeah, this song always sucked, don't get me wrong. I'm just glad CS gave us this video, otherwise, the song would be COMPLETELY and UTTERLY irredeemable, to this day.
